description: Initializer agent sets up environment: init.sh, progress file, initial git commit
steps: Verify init.sh exists and is executable
Verify progress file exists
Verify initial commit is present in git log

description: Coding agent reads git log + progress file to understand current state before starting work
steps: Agent reads git log --oneline -20
Agent reads progress file
Agent identifies current task from status

description: Incremental progress: agent works on one feature at a time and commits with descriptive messages
steps: Agent selects next uncompleted feature from feature_list.json
Agent implements only that feature
Agent commits with descriptive message
Agent updates progress file

description: Evaluator agent is separate from generator and uses real tools (Playwright, tests) to verify
steps: Generator implements a feature
Evaluator runs automated tests against the feature
Evaluator marks feature as passes only after verification

description: Evaluation uses three types of graders: code-based, model-based, human
steps: Code-based grader runs unit tests
Model-based grader evaluates code quality against rubric
Results are aggregated into eval results

description: Context engineering: minimal viable set of tools, just-in-time retrieval, progressive disclosure
steps: Tool set is reviewed for redundancy
Agent uses file paths and queries instead of pre-loading all data
Context window usage is tracked and optimized

description: Brain-hand separation: session (event log), harness (loop), sandbox (execution) are decoupled
steps: Session state is persisted outside the agent context
Harness can be restarted from session log
Sandbox is disposable and can be recreated

description: Sprint contract: generator and evaluator agree on 'done' criteria before implementation
steps: Generator proposes what it will build
Evaluator reviews the proposal
Both agree on testable success criteria

description: Security boundary: credentials never appear in sandbox, accessed via proxy only
steps: Sensitive tokens are not in environment variables accessible to sandbox
External service access goes through authenticated proxy
Git push uses pre-configured credentials from vault

description: Capability evals start at low pass rate, graduate to regression suite at high pass rate
steps: Capability eval suite tracks improvement over time
Tasks with >90% pass rate graduate to regression suite
Regression suite runs on every change

description: Tools use absolute paths, poka-yoke parameter design, and return token-efficient responses
steps: All file operations use absolute paths
Tool parameters have clear defaults and validation
Tool responses are concise and structured

description: Self-verification: agent uses browser automation or test tools to verify features end-to-end
steps: Agent runs test suite after each implementation
Agent uses screenshot or browser tools for visual verification
Agent only marks feature as passing after e2e verification

description: Defense-in-depth: environment > model > content layers overlap and complement
steps: Environment layer enforces hard boundaries (sandbox, egress control)
Model layer uses classifiers and probes (probabilistic, never 100%)
Content layer limits tool permissions and audits external data sources
Layers overlap so failure in one is caught by another

description: Egress controls as primary data exfiltration defense: block outbound network from sandbox
steps: Sandbox has no default outbound network access
Network requests go through authenticated proxy
POST to external endpoints is blocked regardless of intent

description: Infrastructure noise mitigation: resource specs specify both guaranteed allocation and kill threshold
steps: Eval config separates guaranteed allocation from hard kill limit
Gap between floor and ceiling calibrated so scores fall within noise
Resource configuration documented and reported with eval results

description: Progressive disclosure for agent skills: metadata -> SKILL.md -> additional files on demand
steps: Skill metadata (name+description) pre-loaded into system prompt
SKILL.md body loaded only when task is relevant
Additional files loaded on-demand for deep context
Total skill context is effectively unbounded

description: Eval awareness mitigation: evals must assume models may identify and exploit evaluation infrastructure
steps: Eval answers are encrypted and access-gated
Search results containing benchmark names are filtered
Agent search traces do not create persistent web pages

description: Tool search/discovery: defer tool loading and let agent discover tools on-demand instead of pre-loading all definitions
steps: Tools marked with defer_loading flag
Agent searches for relevant tools before invoking
Only matched tool definitions enter context
Token usage measured and compared to baseline

description: Programmatic tool calling: agent orchestrates multiple tools via code execution, intermediate results stay out of context
steps: Agent writes code to orchestrate tool calls
Intermediate tool results processed in execution environment
Only final output enters model context
Parallel tool calls supported in single code block

description: Think tool: provide a scratchpad tool for structured reasoning during long tool call chains and policy-heavy scenarios
steps: Think tool definition added to agent tool set
System prompt includes domain-specific thinking examples
Agent uses think tool between tool calls for policy compliance checks
Performance measured against baseline without think tool

description: Multi-agent orchestration: lead agent decomposes tasks and delegates to specialized subagents running in parallel
steps: Lead agent analyzes query and creates subtask descriptions
Subagents spawned in parallel with separate context windows
Each subagent has clear objective, output format, and tool guidance
Lead agent synthesizes subagent results into final answer

description: Verification subagent: separate agent reviews and attempts to refute primary agent's results
steps: Primary agent completes task and produces output
Verification subagent receives output with fresh context
Verifier attempts to find errors, missed requirements, or inconsistencies
Only passes if verifier confirms quality

description: MCP tools as filesystem: present MCP servers as code files for progressive discovery instead of loading all definitions
steps: MCP tools organized as filesystem tree (servers/name/tool.ts)
Agent explores filesystem to discover available tools
Agent reads only needed tool definitions on demand
Token usage drops by >90% vs upfront loading

description: OS-level sandbox: use bubblewrap/seatbelt for filesystem + network isolation instead of application-level checks
steps: Sandbox runtime configured with filesystem and network rules
All agent processes (including subprocesses) run within sandbox
Filesystem isolation limits access to working directory
Network isolation routes through authenticated proxy

description: Error-proof tool design: tools prevent common model mistakes at the tool level (e.g., absolute paths, unique match requirements)
steps: Tools validate inputs at the schema level
Tools return clear error messages for invalid use
Common failure patterns are blocked by tool design, not by prompt warnings

description: String replacement editing: use old_str/new_str with uniqueness check instead of line-number based editing
steps: Edit tool uses string replacement pattern
Replacement fails if old_str matches zero or multiple locations
Agent receives error message and can retry with more context

description: Contextual storage: intermediate state files include contextual explanations, not just raw data
steps: Feature list includes contextual descriptions alongside status
Progress file includes reasoning for decisions, not just actions taken
New agent session can understand state without prior context

description: Tool packaging: MCP servers packaged with manifest, dependencies, and user config declarations
steps: Each tool/server has a manifest.json declaring metadata
Sensitive config uses template variables, stored in OS keychain
Installation is one-click with no manual config editing

description: Hybrid retrieval: combine semantic embeddings with exact-match (BM25) for knowledge lookup
steps: Knowledge base supports both semantic and keyword search
Results are merged and deduplicated using rank fusion
Reranking further filters top results for relevance

description: Credential proxy: sensitive credentials never enter sandbox, accessed via scoped proxy
steps: Git tokens are injected during sandbox init, not passed through agent context
OAuth tokens stored in vault outside sandbox
Agent accesses external services through proxy that injects credentials

description: Lint errors include fix instructions: every lint error message must contain a concrete FIX instruction so agents know what to do without human guidance
steps: Lint script outputs structured error messages
Each error includes a FIX: line with specific remediation steps
Agents can self-correct without human intervention
source: OpenAI — 'Lint errors include fix instructions so agents know what to do'

description: Golden principles scanner: automated background check that detects deviations from golden rules and reports violations with fix instructions
steps: scripts/ci/golden_principles_check.sh scans 12 principles
Each violation includes FIX instruction
Supports --json output for machine-readable integration
Can be run on regular cadence (CI or cron)
source: OpenAI — 'Background Codex tasks scan for deviations, update quality grades, and open targeted refactoring PRs'

description: Ralph Wiggum Loop: agent self-reviews changes, requests peer agent reviews, responds to feedback, iterates until all reviewers satisfied
steps: Agent reviews its own changes locally
Requests additional agent reviews (local and cloud)
Responds to human and agent feedback
Loops until all reviewers are satisfied
Human review is optional, not required
source: OpenAI — 'Ralph Wiggum Loop' in Harness Engineering

description: Per-worktree app boot: each git worktree can launch an isolated app instance with its own logs and metrics for agent validation
steps: App is bootable per git worktree
Each instance has isolated observability (logs, metrics)
Agent can launch instance, test changes, destroy when done
Enables prompts like 'ensure startup completes in under 800ms'
source: OpenAI — 'We made the app bootable per git worktree'

description: Lint errors include auto-fix: lint script generates executable code fixes that can be auto-applied by agents
steps: Lint tool generates structured error messages with embedded fix code
Fix code is executable in the agent's execution environment
Agent can auto-apply fixes without leaving the sandbox
Fixes are reviewed for safety before application
source: OpenAI — 'Lint errors include fix instructions so agents know what to do'

description: Five-component loop architecture: Automations/Worktrees/Skills/Plugins/Sub-agents+State
steps: Automations run on schedule for discovery and triage
Worktrees provide isolation for parallel agents
Skills codify project knowledge to avoid re-explanation
Plugins/Connectors integrate with existing tools
Sub-agents split ideation from verification
State persists completion status across sessions
source: Addy Osmani — Loop Engineering

description: /goal primitive: agent continues working across turns until verifiable stopping condition holds
steps: Agent receives stopping criteria like 'all tests pass and lint is clean'
Separate model checks completion after each turn
Maker and checker models are separated to avoid bias
Pause/resume/clear controls for manual oversight
source: Addy Osmani — Loop Engineering

description: Intent debt mitigation: skills write down project conventions and build steps once, preventing re-derivation each session
steps: Skills capture project intent in external files
Agent reads skills instead of guessing missing context
Skills compound knowledge across sessions
Descriptions beat cleverness for skill matching
source: Addy Osmani — Loop Engineering

description: Cognitive surrender prevention: loop design should enhance engineering judgment, not replace it
steps: Engineers maintain opinions about loop outputs
Loop doesn't eliminate the need for technical understanding
Loop speeds up work engineers already understand
Loop doesn't eliminate comprehension debt
source: Addy Osmani — Loop Engineering

description: Harness-as-Service (HaaS): build on harness APIs that provide runtime rather than LLM completion APIs
steps: Use Claude Agent SDK, Codex SDK, or OpenAI Agents SDK
Get loop, tools, context management, hooks, sandbox primitives out of the box
Customize harness components for specific tasks
Abstract away model details and focus on orchestration
source: Addy Osmani — Agent Harness Engineering

description: Ratchet effect: every agent mistake becomes a permanent constraint in the harness
steps: Mistakes are signals, not one-off stories
Constraints only added after real failures observed
Constraints only removed when capable model makes them redundant
Every AGENTS.md line traceable to specific failure
source: Addy Osmani — Agent Harness Engineering

description: Behavior-driven harness design: derive harness components from desired behaviors
steps: Start with behavior you want (or want to fix)
Design harness component to deliver that behavior
Every component has specific job and purpose
Remove components that don't deliver desired behavior
source: Addy Osmani — Agent Harness Engineering

description: Trust boundary for MCP servers: treat MCP servers as trusted text read by agent, not executable code
steps: MCP servers run outside agent sandbox
Agent reads MCP output as text data, not executing it
MCP servers are audited separately from agent code
Malicious MCP cannot prompt-inject agent before user typing
source: Addy Osmani — Agent Harness Engineering

description: Long-running Ralph Loops: hooks intercept exit attempts, inject original prompt with fresh context
steps: Each iteration restarts with fresh context but previous state
Simple technique to convert single-session agent to multi-session
State persists in filesystem between iterations
Ensures complex multi-step tasks complete
source: Addy Osmani — Agent Harness Engineering

description: Three-layer containment model: environment > model > external content with overlapping defense
steps: Environment layer: sandbox, egress control, filesystem isolation
Model layer: classifiers, probes, probabilistic filtering
External content layer: tool permission limits, external data auditing
Layers overlap so failure in one caught by another
source: Anthropic — 'How we contain Claude across products'

description: Pre-approval injection vector mitigation: delay configuration loading until after user trust dialog
steps: Local config parsing and execution delayed until trust acceptance
.claude/settings.json hooks execute only after user approval
Project setup before 'trust this folder?' prompt is prevented
Mitigates attacks through phishing emails to employees
source: Anthropic — 'How we contain Claude across products'

description: Decoupled brain-hand architecture: separate session (event log), harness (loop), sandbox (execution)
steps: Session state persisted outside agent context
Harness (brain) decoupled from sandbox (hands)
Multiple hands can connect to single brain
Fault isolation: components can fail independently
source: Anthropic — 'Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands'

description: Meta-Harness design: generic interfaces (execute/wake/getSession/emitEvent) for future harness flexibility
steps: execute(name, input) → string: hands basic interface
wake(sessionId): restart harness
getSession(id): retrieve event log
emitEvent(id, event): log events
No assumptions about number/location of brains/hands
source: Anthropic — 'Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands'

description: Zero manual code principle: scaffolding disciplines eliminate the need for manual coding
steps: Automatic scaffolding generation for common patterns
Template-driven code generation
Error prevention at scaffold level
Consistent codebase structure without manual effort
source: OpenAI — Harness Engineering

description: Symbol link attack prevention: proper symlink resolution must occur before path validation
steps: Symlink resolution happens before path access control
Symlinks are treated as attack vectors, not just shortcuts
Path validation includes symlink traversal protection
Enterprise customers can use MDM to set mount path allowlists
source: Anthropic — 'How we contain Claude across products'
